Summary
Microsoft Certified Professional with 11 years of experience in database administration on MS SQL Server 2016/2014/2012/2008
R2/ 2008. Proficient in managing complex database environments, performance tuning, and ensuring high availability.
Proven ability to lead DBA teams, provide critical support, and drive system improvements. Experienced with Azure
cloud services and various database technologies.

Lead Database Administrator Full-time

data
redundancy and uptime.

prehensive database health checks.

8, Oracle 10g / 11g, Netezza, PostgreSQL,

Amal Rashid is a Microsoft Certified Professional with 11 years of experience in database administration on MS SQL Server, proficient in managing complex database environments and leading DBA teams. She can be contacted at amal.rashid@email.com, +20 101 234 5678, and through her online profiles on GitHub and LinkedIn.
Amal Rashid obtained a Masters of Science in Computer Science from Indira College, Pune University in 2010, located in Pune, India.

From June 2022 to the present, Amal Rashid has been working as a Lead Database Administrator at Ensono in Cairo, Egypt, following a project acquisition from Wipro Technologies. She manages and optimizes complex database environments, ensuring high performance and security.

From June 2017 to May 2022, Amal Rashid worked as a Lead Database Administrator at Wipro Technologies in Giza, Egypt, where she led a team of 5 SQL DBAs and managed data center services. She also provided mentorship and implemented high availability solutions like Always On Availability Groups.

During her time at Wipro Technologies, Amal Rashid performed performance tuning, troubleshooting, and root cause analysis for high severity incidents. She also managed incident, change, and problem management processes and contributed to PCI scan report remediation.
From February 2015 to May 2017, Amal Rashid worked as a Senior Database Administrator at Mphasis in Alexandria, Egypt, administering MS SQL Server and managing high availability solutions.

The individual maintained system security, compliance, and stability by executing SQL Server patching activities. They also generated and analyzed daily reports on cluster failover, backups, and the status of Availability Groups, Log Shipping, and Mirroring, as well as server disk space utilization. They efficiently resolved service requests, incidents, and critical issues to minimize downtime and participated in quarterly Disaster Recovery (DR) activities to ensure database recoverability and business continuity.

The individual collaborated with the Windows team during patching operations for Availability Group/mirror/cluster failover/failback and comprehensive database health checks. They also conducted extensive performance tuning to optimize database queries and enhance overall system responsiveness.

From July 2012 to January 2015, the individual worked as a Database Administrator at Synechron Technologies Pvt. Ltd in Cairo, Egypt, providing support for SQL Server, Oracle 10g, and Netezza databases. They performed installation, upgrade, and administration tasks for MS SQL Server 2008 R2/2008, conducted capacity planning, and troubleshooted database alerts and issues. They also implemented and maintained log shipping and database mirroring for disaster recovery and completed training on Oracle 11g Architecture and SQL Server best practices.

From July 2011 to June 2012, the individual worked as a Junior Database Administrator at Mastek Ltd in Giza, Egypt, assisting with SQL Server 2008 R2/2008 installation, upgrade, and administration. They performed routine database backup and restore operations, conducted MS SQL Server audits, and supported database mirroring, replication, log shipping, and clustering environments. They also provided support for User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and Production (PROD) environments and reviewed deployment documents.

From July 2010 to June 2011, the individual worked on the Dairy ERP System Customization project at PP Software and Systems Ltd in Cairo, Egypt, designing, coding, and testing customized Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system modules for the dairy industry. They developed and integrated Master Data Management and Procurement modules using Core Java and PostgreSQL.
The individual holds a Microsoft Certified Professional certification and is also Microsoft Azure certified.

A range of tools and software are mentioned, including MS LiteSpeed, Idera SQL Safe, SSMS, Upgrade Advisor, SQL Server Profiler, SCOM, Diagnostic Manager, Remedy, Jira, Infopacc, Tivoli TDP Backup Tool, SQL Pack, and Holmes BOTS.
The text details high availability solutions such as Always On Availability Groups, Database Mirroring, Log Shipping, Replication, and Clustering.
Database management tasks are listed, including Performance Tuning, Installation, Upgrade, Migration, Patching, Backup/Restore, Capacity Planning, Troubleshooting, and Audits.
Cloud platforms mentioned include Microsoft Azure, specifically its VM, Storage, Network, and AD components.
The text lists methodologies such as Incident Management, Change Management, Problem Management, DR Activity, RCA, and SIP.
Programming skill mentioned is Core Java.

Egypt, a land steeped in ancient history, also pulses with a modern technological heart. Within its bustling cities – Cairo, Giza, and Alexandria – a network of skilled professionals quietly ensures the smooth operation of critical data systems. This nation serves as a key location for global IT services, attracting companies like Wipro, Mphasis, Synechron, Mastek, PP Software and Systems, and Ensono. 

It’s a place where careers are built and refined over years of dedicated service. Consider Amal Rashid, a Lead Database Administrator whose expertise has been honed across multiple organizations within Egypt. Starting as a Junior Administrator in 2011, she quickly demonstrated a talent for managing complex database environments, mastering SQL Server, Oracle, and Netezza. Her journey reflects a commitment to continuous learning, from early work with log shipping and mirroring for disaster recovery to implementing high availability solutions like Always On Availability Groups. 

She’s a leader, having guided teams of DBAs, a mentor, and a problem-solver, consistently ensuring high performance and security. Her experience spans project acquisitions, data center management, and the customization of ERP systems for specialized industries like dairy production – a testament to Egypt’s diverse technological landscape. Egypt, therefore, isn’t just a geographical location; it’s a thriving hub for technological expertise, a place where professionals like Amal Rashid contribute to the global flow of information.

SQL Server is a robust and critical foundation, a digital stronghold demanding constant vigilance. It’s a system built for resilience, requiring meticulous upkeep – patching, monitoring, and proactive defense against disruption. This isn’t a passive component; it’s a dynamic entity whose health directly reflects the stability of the businesses it supports. 

Its core function is data management, but its true value lies in ensuring that data remains accessible, secure, and recoverable. It’s a guardian of business continuity, evidenced by its capacity for complex configurations like Availability Groups, Log Shipping, and Mirroring – intricate systems designed to withstand failure. Daily reports detailing cluster failovers, backup status, and disk space are its vital signs, constantly scrutinized for anomalies. 

Experienced hands, like those at Synechron Technologies, understand its nuances. They’ve wrestled with installations and upgrades of versions like 2008 R2 and 2008, planned for growth, and tirelessly hunted down the root causes of database alerts. They’ve built redundancy into its architecture through log shipping and mirroring, preparing for the inevitable. SQL Server isn’t simply *supported*; it’s actively *maintained* – a constant cycle of preventative care and rapid response to ensure its unwavering performance. It’s a demanding system, but in capable hands, it’s a bedrock of operational reliability.

Amal Rashid is a seasoned and certified Microsoft Professional, a lead database administrator with eleven years dedicated to the intricacies of MS SQL Server. She’s a problem solver at heart, equally comfortable diving into performance tuning and root cause analysis as she is leading and mentoring teams. Her career trajectory, spanning roles at Mphasis, Wipro Technologies, and currently Ensono in Cairo, Egypt, demonstrates a consistent climb in responsibility and expertise – a journey that began with a Masters of Science in Computer Science from Indira College, Pune, in 2010. 

Initially honing her skills administering SQL Server at Mphasis, Amal quickly rose to lead DBA teams at Wipro, where she managed data center services for five DBAs and championed high availability solutions like Always On Availability Groups. Following a project acquisition, she brought her expertise to Ensono, continuing to optimize complex database environments and ensure robust security. Throughout her career, she’s navigated the demands of incident, change, and problem management, and contributed to critical PCI scan report remediation. 

Amal isn’t simply a technician; she’s a proactive leader, a mentor, and a dedicated professional with a proven ability to maintain database integrity and performance under pressure. She can be reached at amal.rashid@email.com, +20 101 234 5678, or through her profiles on GitHub and LinkedIn.

Wipro Technologies, a global technology services and consulting company, operates with a significant presence in Giza, Egypt. Known for undertaking large-scale projects and providing comprehensive data center services, Wipro once employed Amal Rashid as a Lead Database Administrator. For five years, Rashid spearheaded a team of five SQL DBAs, responsible for the performance, security, and high availability of critical database environments. She was a key figure in resolving high-severity incidents, expertly performing performance tuning and root cause analysis. Beyond reactive problem-solving, Rashid proactively implemented solutions like Always On Availability Groups and managed vital ITIL processes – incident, change, and problem management – while also contributing to PCI compliance. However, a portion of Wipro’s project portfolio, including the work Rashid oversaw, was subsequently acquired by Ensono, leading to Rashid’s transition to a Lead DBA role within the acquiring company in Cairo. Wipro, therefore, appears as a provider of outsourced technology services, capable of managing complex database infrastructure, but also one that strategically adjusts its project holdings through acquisitions.

Giza, a city steeped in ancient history, also pulses with the energy of modern technology. It’s a place where the shadows of the pyramids fall upon data centers and the skills of database administrators are highly sought after. Within this Egyptian hub, Amal Rashid has built a career dedicated to the smooth operation of critical data systems. Starting as a Junior Database Administrator at Mastek Ltd in 2011, she quickly gained hands-on experience with the intricacies of SQL Server – installation, upgrades, backups, audits, and the complex architectures of mirroring, replication, and clustering. This foundational period saw her supporting both the rigorous demands of User Acceptance Testing and the live pressures of Production environments, honing her eye for detail through deployment document review. 

By 2017, Rashid had risen to become a Lead Database Administrator at Wipro Technologies, also located within Giza. Here, she took the reins of a five-person SQL DBA team, responsible for the vital data center services underpinning the company’s operations. A natural mentor, she fostered growth within her team while simultaneously architecting and implementing robust high availability solutions, notably leveraging Always On Availability Groups to ensure uninterrupted service. Giza, therefore, isn’t just a location on a map for Rashid; it’s the backdrop to a career defined by technical expertise, leadership, and a commitment to data integrity. It’s a city where ancient wonders coexist with the demands of a digital world, and Rashid is a key player in bridging that gap.

MS SQL Server 2008 R2 is a robust and widely-deployed relational database management system, a cornerstone of data infrastructure for many organizations in the early 2010s and beyond. It’s a mature platform known for its reliability and comprehensive feature set, offering tools for data storage, retrieval, and analysis. While newer versions have emerged, 2008 R2 remains a significant presence, particularly in legacy systems. 

Its strengths lie in its support for transactional processing, business intelligence, and reporting services. Features like T-SQL, stored procedures, and triggers allow for complex data manipulation and automation. Disaster recovery options, such as log shipping and database mirroring – techniques honed by professionals like Amal Rashid during her time at Synechron Technologies – were integral to ensuring data protection and business continuity. 

Rashid’s experience highlights the practical application of 2008 R2; she expertly managed its installation, upgrades, and ongoing administration, demonstrating a deep understanding of its capabilities and limitations. Though she also worked with Oracle and Netezza, her proficiency with SQL Server 2008 R2 was a key component of her role, supporting critical database environments and troubleshooting complex issues. It’s a system that, in skilled hands, proved capable of handling demanding workloads and providing a stable foundation for data-driven operations.

Cairo hums with a relentless energy, a backdrop to the focused career of Amal Rashid. A city firmly rooted in Egypt, it’s a place where ancient history brushes against the demands of a modern, technological world – a duality mirrored in Amal’s own professional journey. For over a decade, Cairo has been the constant geographic anchor for her expertise in the intricate world of database administration. 

She currently thrives as a Lead Database Administrator at Ensono, a role secured following an acquisition from Wipro Technologies, where she orchestrates the performance and security of complex database environments. Before that, Synechron Technologies Pvt. Ltd. provided her with a solid foundation, from 2012 to 2015, honing her skills across SQL Server, Oracle, and Netezza – a period marked by meticulous installation, upgrades, and proactive troubleshooting. She’s a planner, anticipating capacity needs and building robust disaster recovery systems through log shipping and mirroring. 

Her path began in 2010 at PP Software and Systems Ltd., where she delved into the specific needs of the dairy industry, crafting customized ERP modules with Core Java and PostgreSQL. This early experience demonstrates a talent for not just maintaining systems, but *building* them, tailoring technology to the unique demands of a business. 

Throughout her career, Cairo has been more than just a location; it’s the hub from which Amal connects her technical prowess to a diverse range of projects and companies – Ensono, Synechron, and PP Software all finding their base within the city’s dynamic landscape. It’s a city that witnesses her growth, a silent partner in her dedication to data integrity and system optimization.

Amal Rashid is a seasoned database professional, distinguished as a Microsoft Certified Professional with eleven years dedicated to the intricacies of MS SQL Server. She doesn’t simply *manage* databases; she orchestrates them, expertly navigating complex environments and guiding DBA teams with practiced ease. Beyond the foundational MCP, Amal’s skillset extends into the cloud, boasting Microsoft Azure certification – a testament to her commitment to staying at the forefront of data management technology. She’s a problem-solver, a leader, and a dedicated practitioner, readily accessible via amal.rashid@email.com or +20 101 234 5678, and actively engaged within the developer community through her presence on GitHub and LinkedIn. She represents a blend of proven expertise and forward-thinking adaptability in the world of data administration.

Azure is the boundless digital estate of Microsoft, a sprawling cloud platform built for a world demanding scalability and flexibility. It isn’t merely a collection of servers, but a dynamic ecosystem – a foundational layer for innovation. Think of it as a vast, adaptable infrastructure, offering everything from virtual machines (VMs) – the digital equivalents of powerful, customizable computers – to robust and secure storage solutions, capable of safeguarding data at any scale. 

Its network capabilities are equally impressive, weaving connections across the globe with speed and reliability, allowing applications to communicate seamlessly. And underpinning it all is Azure Active Directory (AD), a sophisticated identity and access management system, ensuring only the right people – or processes – gain entry. 

Azure isn’t a single entity, but a suite of interconnected services. It’s the place where developers build and deploy applications, where businesses store and analyze data, and where organizations transform their IT infrastructure. It’s a proving ground for those seeking Microsoft certification, a testament to expertise in navigating its complexities. It’s a constantly evolving landscape, always expanding, always adapting, always ready to power the next generation of digital experiences. It’s the backbone of modern cloud computing, and a key to unlocking future possibilities.

Performance Tuning is a critical discipline, a meticulous art practiced within the realm of database management. It’s the focused effort to coax optimal speed and efficiency from a system, ensuring it doesn’t merely *function*, but *performs*. Often called upon in moments of crisis – high severity incidents demanding immediate attention – it’s a proactive measure as much as a reactive one. Individuals like Amal Rashid, honed their skills through this practice at companies like Wipro Technologies, becoming adept at identifying bottlenecks and resolving underlying issues. 

But Performance Tuning isn’t a solitary endeavor. It exists as a vital component within a broader suite of database responsibilities. It walks hand-in-hand with Installation, Upgrade, Migration, Patching, Backup/Restore, Capacity Planning, Troubleshooting, and Audits – each informing and influencing the others. A successful installation might *require* subsequent tuning; a seamless upgrade *depends* on anticipating performance impacts. It’s a constant cycle of refinement, ensuring the database remains responsive and reliable, capable of handling increasing demands and maintaining data integrity. It’s the silent guardian of system health, working behind the scenes to deliver a smooth, efficient user experience.

MS SQL Server 2008 is a robust and widely-deployed relational database management system, a cornerstone of data infrastructure for many organizations in the early 2010s and beyond. It’s a mature platform, known for its reliability and comprehensive feature set, capable of handling everything from small business applications to large-scale enterprise solutions. While newer versions have emerged, SQL Server 2008 remains a significant presence, particularly in legacy systems. 

Its strengths lie in its transactional processing capabilities, robust security features, and integration with other Microsoft products. It supports a full range of database features including stored procedures, triggers, views, and indexes, allowing for complex data manipulation and efficient querying. Disaster recovery options like log shipping and database mirroring, as demonstrated by professionals like Amal Rashid during her time at Synechron Technologies, were key components of its appeal, ensuring data protection and business continuity. 

Though superseded by later iterations, SQL Server 2008’s architecture continues to influence modern database design. Individuals like Amal Rashid, a Microsoft Certified Professional with over a decade of experience, honed their skills administering and maintaining this platform, becoming adept at installation, upgrades, capacity planning, and troubleshooting – skills readily transferable to newer SQL Server versions. It represents a pivotal point in database technology, a workhorse that powered countless applications and laid the groundwork for the sophisticated data management systems of today.

Cluster Failover is the silent guardian of database availability, a critical process meticulously observed and proactively managed. It’s not a thing *done*, but a state constantly watched for, a potential event prepared for. This individual’s watch centered on ensuring seamless transitions when that event occurred – a shift in responsibility from one server to another, maintaining uninterrupted service. They were the analyst of its patterns, generating daily reports detailing its occurrences alongside the health of related systems: Availability Groups, mirroring configurations, and regular backups. 

Their work wasn’t simply reactive; it was preventative. Patching activities, executed with a keen eye for security and compliance, were vital to *avoiding* unwanted failovers. When patching *did* necessitate a shift, collaboration with the Windows team was key, ensuring smooth failover and failback procedures. Beyond the immediate event, they were a performance architect, tuning queries and optimizing the system to minimize the *need* for failover in the first place. 

This individual wasn’t just fixing problems; they were safeguarding business continuity. Quarterly Disaster Recovery exercises were a testament to this, proving the system’s ability to recover even from significant disruption. They were the first line of defense against downtime, resolving incidents and critical issues with efficiency, and a constant monitor of disk space – a seemingly small detail with potentially large consequences for failover capacity. Ultimately, Cluster Failover, under their care, represented a promise of resilience, a quiet assurance that data remained accessible, no matter what.

Log Shipping is a steadfast, if somewhat understated, guardian of data integrity and business continuity. Not flashy like its newer cousin, Always On Availability Groups, it’s a reliable workhorse, diligently backing up transaction logs and shipping them to a secondary, standby server. This process ensures a readily available replica, poised to take over should disaster strike the primary. 

Experienced hands – Database Administrators like those at Synechron and Mastek – have long favored Log Shipping as a cornerstone of disaster recovery plans. It’s a proven technique, often implemented alongside other high availability solutions like mirroring, replication, and clustering, forming a layered defense against data loss. These administrators routinely monitor its health, alongside those other systems, analyzing daily reports to confirm successful backups and failover readiness. 

While newer technologies offer more automated features, Log Shipping remains a vital skill, a testament to its enduring effectiveness. It’s a practical, time-tested method for maintaining a recoverable database environment, a silent partner in ensuring minimal downtime and the smooth operation of critical business functions. It’s a foundational element in a comprehensive strategy for safeguarding data, a legacy solution still very much in demand.
